# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.123.0/http/server.ts";
import { parse } from "https://deno.land/std@0.123.0/flags/mod.ts";
import { existsSync, readFileSync } from "https://deno.land/std@0.123.0/fs/mod.ts";

const PORT = 80;
const HOST = "localhost";

interface ModuleConfig {
  path: string;
}

async function loadModule(path: string): Promise<any> {
  const modulePath = `${Deno.cwd()}/${path}`;
  if (!existsSync(modulePath)) {
    throw new Error(`Module not found at ${modulePath}`);
  }
  return import(modulePath);
}

async function handleRequest(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const pathname = url.pathname;
  const queryParams = parse(url.searchParams);

  let moduleConfig: ModuleConfig | undefined;
  if (queryParams.has("module")) {
    moduleConfig = {
      path: queryParams.get("module") as string,
    };
  } else if (process.env.MODULE_PATH) {
    moduleConfig = {
      path: process.env.MODULE_PATH,
    };
  }

  if (!moduleConfig) {
    return new Response("No module specified", { status: 400 });
  }

  const module = await loadModule(moduleConfig.path);
  if (typeof module !== "function") {
    return new Response(`Invalid module at ${moduleConfig.path}`, { status: 500 });
  }

  try {
    const result = await module();
    return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), { status: 200 });
  } catch (err) {
    console.error(err);
    return new Response("Internal server error", { status: 500 });
  }
}

serve({ hostname: HOST, port: PORT }, handleRequest);
